Article Title: SC: Officials of 3 states liable for Chambal mining
Article Text: NEW DELHI : Taking suo motu cognisance of destruction of wildlife habitats in the National Chambal Sanctuary, Supreme Court on Friday issued notice to the govts of Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh and observed that their officials should be made liable for the damage as they failed to prevent illegal sand mining in the area.A bench of Justices Vikram Nath and Sandeep Mehta expressed concern over the damage being done to the sanctuary and said all aspects had to be examined.“The officials of the departments concerned, that is, forest, mining and water resources as well as police authorities of the states of Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh by their lethargy and inaction would be liable to be held vicariously liable for aiding and abetting the destruction of these precious habitats by allowing illegal sand mining to continue,” the bench said.
Article Summary: Justices Vikram Nath and Sandeep Mehta expressed concern over the damage being done to the sanctuary. “The officials of the departments concerned, that is, forest, mining and water resources as well as police authorities of the states of Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh by their lethargy and inaction would be liable to be held vicariously liable,” the bench said.

Article Title: Omar Abdullah: Sweeping transfers by EC only happen in non-BJP-governed states: Omar
Article Text:
Omar Abdullah (File photo)

Watch Eid Politics Heats Up Bengal: Mamata Banerjee Attacks BJP, Suvendu Adhikari Pushes Sanatan Narrative Eid Politics Heats Up Bengal: Mamata Banerjee Attacks BJP, Suvendu Adhikari Pushes Sanatan Narrative

NEW DELHI: J&K CM and National Conference leader Omar Abdullah on Friday came out strongly in support of his West Bengal counterpart Mamata Banerjee on the issue of Election Commission transferring officials in poll-bound Bengal, saying these “sweeping transfers only happen in non-BJP-governed states”.“Bengal will once again prove that officers don’t win elections for political parties, the leaders of political parties do,” said Omar, an INDIA bloc ally, reacting to Mamata’s Thursday post on X in which she alleged that the manner in which EC has “singled out and targeted Bengal is not just unprecedented but deeply alarming”.Even before the formal notification of elections, more than 50 senior officials, including chief secretary, home secretary and DGP, were “summarily and arbitrarily removed”, she alleged.Sharing Mamata’s post, Omar said, “No amount of effort by EC to gerrymander will change the results. Come counting day Mamata didi will win a thumping majority.”Highlighting that various parties have come forward to support Mamata, her party TMC shared a video clip of SP chief Akhilesh Yadav, while talking to reporters, saying, “I want to congratulate respected Mamata Banerjee in advance and said the people of Bengal will vote in her favour.” He said that the conspiracy led by BJP to defeat her, including the recent transfer of officials, will not work.“It is people who vote not the officials,” Akhilesh said.On Thursday, after Mamata’s post, AAP supremo Arvind Kejriwal had posted, “BJP has turned EC into its weapon to win elections through deceit. What is happening today in Bengal is exactly what happened in the Delhi elections as well.” “Names were struck off the voter list, the police administration gave free rein to BJP’s thuggery, and the entire administration was engaged in ensuring BJP’s victory. The very fabric of democracy was torn apart,” he added.AAP, which is not part of the INDIA bloc, had signed the opposition’s notice, piloted by TMC, seeking a motion for removal of CEC Gyanesh Kumar that was submitted to both Houses of Parliament last week.Shiv Sena (UBT)’s Sanjay Raut said on X that “defeating Mamata didi in West Bengal is not just difficult, it’s impossible! That’s why BJP is playing dirty by hiding behind the shield of EC”.
Article Summary: J&K CM Omar Abdullah came out strongly in support of his West Bengal counterpart Mamata Banerjee on the issue of Election Commission transferring officials in poll-bound Bengal. “Bengal will once again prove that officers don’t win elections for political parties, the leaders of political parties do,” said Omar.

Article Title: Punjab Warehousing Official Suicide: ‘Kha layi celphos tuhade yaar ne’: Watch officer’s last video before suicide in Punjab; ex-minister Bhullar, father among 3 booked
Article Text:
(With agency inputs)





CHANDIGARH: Police have registered a case against former Punjab minister Laljit Singh Bhullar in connection with the alleged suicide of a Punjab State Warehousing Corporation official, officials said on Sunday.Gagandeep Singh Randhawa, district manager of the corporation in Amritsar, allegedly died by suicide after consuming a poisonous substance.Bhullar’s father, Sukhdev Singh Bhullar, and his personal assistant, Dilbag Singh, have also been named in the case.An FIR has been lodged under Sections 108 (abetment of suicide), 351(3) (criminal intimidation), and 3(5) (common intention) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita.The case was registered at Ranjit Avenue Police Station in Amritsar on Saturday night based on a complaint filed by Randhawa’s wife, Upinder Kaur.On Saturday, Bhullar resigned from the state cabinet after chief minister Bhagwant Mann asked him to step down amid allegations of abetment of suicide.A video that surfaced on social media the same day purportedly shows Randhawa—who also held additional charge of Patti—claiming he had consumed Celphos and accusing the transport minister of harassment."Kha layi 'celphos' tuhade yaar ne. Minister Laljit Bhullar de dar ton. Hun nahi main bachda," (Your friend has eaten Celphos because of fear of minister Laljit Bhullar. I will not survive now)," Randhawa says.Bhullar, the MLA from Patti in Tarn Taran, dismissed the allegations against him as baseless.Earlier, chief minister Bhagwant Mann said he had directed the chief secretary to conduct an impartial probe and asked Bhullar to step down to ensure a fair investigation.Mann also asserted that no negligence would be tolerated and said Bhullar’s transport and prison portfolios would be reassigned to another minister.Following the emergence of the video, opposition leaders targeted the AAP government and demanded strict action against Bhullar.They alleged that Bhullar had pressured Randhawa to award a warehouse tender to his father.He was also accused of recording a video of Randhawa and forcing him to admit to accepting a Rs 10 lakh bribe from another party.Another allegation claimed that on March 13, Randhawa was called to Bhullar’s residence, where he was allegedly assaulted and humiliated.
Article Summary: Gagandeep Singh Randhawa, district manager of the corporation in Amritsar, allegedly died by suicide after consuming a poisonous substance. Police have registered a case against former Punjab minister Laljit Singh Bhullar in connection with the alleged suicide of a Punjab State Warehousing Corporation official.

Article Title: ‘Consumed poison out of fear’: District manager’s suicide rocks Punjab; minister resigns after being named in video
Article Text:
Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann (L) Transport and jails minister Laljit Singh Bhullar (R)





CHANDIGARH: Punjab chief minister Bhagwant Mann asked transport and jails minister Laljit Singh Bhullar to hand in his resignation on Saturday after the district manager of Punjab State Warehousing Corporation, Gagandeep Singh Randhawa, died by suicide in Amritsar and left behind a video purportedly blaming the minister.Bhullar resigned but denied the allegations, calling them "completely baseless and false". Opposition parties demand Bhullar's arrest and a CBI probe.Randhawa's 12-second video shows him purportedly saying in Punjabi: "Your friend has consumed poison due to fear of minister Laljit Bhullar. I won't survive."A political storm erupted. The opposition alleged that Bhullar harassed and intimidated the officer and tried to force him to award warehousing tenders to Bhullar's father.They claim Randhawa was assaulted at the minister's residence and pressured into recording a separate video admitting to a Rs 10 lakh bribe to award the tender to another party — claims that are yet to be officially confirmed.With assembly elections less than a year away, AAP govt moved swiftly into damage control.Mann said, "The district manager's suicide came to my notice. It is very sad, and we sympathise with his family.The reasons for the suicide are a matter of investigation. To ensure an impartial probe, we have asked the minister to resign…. No one should be able to influence the probe.""Any action that leads to someone's death is not to be tolerated. We did not enter politics or make parties for this purpose. No official should be pushed so much that he gives up his life," he asserted, and ordered chief secretary KAP Sinha to conduct a fair probe. "We will take appropriate action after that. As of now, I have taken the biggest possible action by asking the minister to resign. His portfolio will be given to someone else soon," said Mann.Bhullar is AAP's MLA from Patti in Tarn Taran district. Randhawa had additional charge of Patti. This is not the first time Bhullar is in the centre of a storm. In April 2024, he faced backlash and was forced to apologise after making derogatory remarks against specific communities at a political rally in Patti.Randhawa's family asked for a CBI probe. His uncle Amarjeet Singh said the family had been under "immense stress". He alleged that Randhawa had to scatter his family at different places after receiving threats that gangsters would abduct them and kill him if he did not comply. "He was a good person. What we know is that he was assaulted and hit on the head. He could not bear this humiliation," he said.Opposition parties ripped into the AAP-led govt. SAD's Bikram Singh Majithia alleged that Randhawa was summoned to the minister's residence on March 13 and assaulted. "Bhullar forced Randhawa to record a video at gunpoint that he had taken a bribe. He was unable to bear this bullying and humiliation. There are several tender applications in the name of Bhullar's father."Amritsar MP Gurjit Singh Aujla of Congress demanded that a police case be registered against Bhullar and urged the authorities to seize CCTV footage from the minister's residence to verify the reports of the alleged March 13 incident.Bhullar rejected the allegations, posting on his Facebook page: "Our party always stands by the truth and supports what is right. Regarding the accusations made against me, I want a proper investigation so that the truth can come out. For this reason, until this probe is completed, I request the party and our chief minister to accept my resignation as minister. I do not want my position to become a hindrance in this matter. I want a fair investigation to take place. Consequently, I am sending my resignation to the chief minister so that the investigation into this case can proceed correctly."Describing Bhullar's resignation as a "drama", Majithia demanded a CBI probe into the case. Randhawa's post-mortem should be conducted at PGI-Chandigarh or AIIMS-Delhi, he said, urging the Chief Justice of Punjab and Haryana high court to take suo motu cognisance and "ensure justice".
Article Summary: Punjab transport and jails minister Laljit Singh Bhullar resigns. Gagandeep Singh Randhawa, district manager of Punjab State Warehousing Corporation, died by suicide in Amritsar. He left behind a video purportedly blaming the minister for his death. Opposition parties demand Bhuller's arrest and a CBI probe.
